body_txt: Not all Applied Phlebotinum can be Made of Indestructium . But some phlebotina have the next-best thing: the non-living equivalent to a Healing Factor . These devices or objects can, given time and perhaps a little more , return to a perfect state without the need for repair work. There are four basic ways for this to work: The explanation for this ability can of course vary from anything like magic to nanomachines . And thanks to advances in nanotechnology and metallurgy, this property is becoming increasingly plausible in Real Life . Already metal panels exist that, when deformed, gradually regain their original shape. Regeneration isn't always a good thing, however. It can be what makes a Clingy MacGuffin or Sentient Phlebotinum reappear when it's seemingly been destroyed, and make that Artifact of Doom all the harder to get rid of.
